dashboard.title: Welcome to the dashboard
dashboard.body: Here you can see your account balances, the balance curve, and your spending by category. Click a category in the donut chart to filter transactions.

imports.title: Import your statements
imports.body: Drop an OFX, CSV, or PDF file. The first time a bank's PDF is imported, a wizard asks you to mark the amount/date/description zones — later imports are automatic.

transactions.title: Search, fix, split
transactions.body: Search ignores accents and case. You can split a transaction into several sub-lines, edit a category inline, or select multiple transactions to delete them all at once.

rules.title: Automatic categorization
rules.body: Rules apply to new imports and can be re-applied retroactively without overwriting your manual categories. From the Sort tab, you can create a rule from a keyword in one click.

budgets.title: Monthly budget tracking
budgets.body: For each expense category, set a planned amount and track the variance in real time. Categories over budget turn red.

accounts.title: Your accounts
accounts.body: Add a checking account, savings account, PEA… The opening balance is required: every balance is computed from there. Locked funds (PEA, term deposit) are kept separate from the available amount.

data.title: Backup and restore
data.body: Export a full backup (accounts, transactions, checkpoints, splits) and re-import it on another install or to restore.

0.title: Welcome to Athena
0.body: Athena is a self-hosted personal accounting app. Your banking data never leaves your network.

1.title: Create your accounts
1.body: Start by adding your bank accounts under "Accounts". The opening balance and opening date are the basis for every calculation.

2.title: Import your statements
2.body: From "Data › Imports", drop your OFX, CSV, or PDF files. Duplicates are detected automatically.

3.title: Analyze your spending
3.body: The dashboard shows your balance and spending by category. You can then set monthly budgets if you'd like.
